markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's decision to delay Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, after earlier shifts from Fall 2025 and May 2026, has extended the timeline and intensified trader focus on pre-release catalysts. Recent leaks pointing to imminent pre-order listings and a potential third trailer have heightened buzz around marketing momentum, while Take-Two's upcoming May earnings call looms as a key checkpoint for any further updates. Geopolitical developments, including signals of progress toward a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire, dominate current market-implied odds as the most probable near-term resolution before launch. This dynamic reflects how prolonged AAA development cycles create windows for unrelated real-world events to intersect with pop-culture anticipation, with traders weighing confirmed industry signals against broader cultural timelines.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' official delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, announced in November 2025 after an earlier shift from the May 26 target, has locked in overwhelming trader consensus that the open-world title will miss any June 2026 window. Successive postponements for additional polish, confirmed by both Rockstar and parent Take-Two Interactive, align with the studio's history of extended development cycles on major releases like Grand Theft Auto V. Pre-order leaks surfacing this week for a May 18 window reflect only retail preparation ahead of the fall launch, not an accelerated release. With the date now firmly in late 2026, realistic upset scenarios remain limited to an unprecedented last-minute surprise, an outcome traders view as negligible given the publisher's fiscal guidance and earnings timeline.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s New York rape retrial, declared after jurors deadlocked on May 15, has sharply tilted trader sentiment toward no additional prison time. With the charge now in limbo and prosecutors granted 30 days to decide on a potential fourth trial, the market assigns an 89.3% implied probability to that outcome, reflecting the high bar for securing a conviction after two prior hung juries and the 2024 appeals-court reversal of his original Manhattan sentence. Weinstein’s separate 16-year Los Angeles conviction continues to keep him incarcerated at Rikers Island, yet the absence of a fresh guilty verdict limits upside risk for traders betting against extended time behind bars. Upcoming developments, including any plea negotiations or renewed prosecution signals, will determine whether momentum shifts.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes lead the 2026 Stanley Cup market with nearly identical implied probabilities, reflecting their elite regular-season form, balanced scoring depth, and strong special-teams play. The Avalanche rely on veteran core experience and power-play efficiency, while the Hurricanes counter with elite goaltending, penalty-kill structure, and consistent defensive play. The Vegas Golden Knights trail with championship pedigree and roster stability, whereas the Montreal Canadiens and Buffalo Sabres remain longer shots powered by young talent and recent momentum gains. This tight clustering at the top illustrates the high parity among contenders as the postseason nears.

This tight clustering at the top illustrates the high parity among contenders as the postseason nears.

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France and Spain lead the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market at similar implied probabilities because both sides combine proven international pedigree with exceptional squad depth just weeks before kickoff. Spain’s Euro 2024 title and extended unbeaten streak underpin trader confidence, while France’s attacking options and experience from recent finals keep them in lockstep. England sits a step behind on the back of improved results under Thomas Tuchel, though the three nations remain tightly bunched due to comparable recent form, balanced schedules, and the absence of any single dominant force. This parity among top European contenders, reinforced by strong qualifying campaigns, sustains the narrow spread in current pricing.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
